exceptions.reason: Select renders a native <select>, so the combobox role is implicit rather than authored. Verify when level 2 lands.
Same native <select>: the browser tracks and exposes the popup's open/closed state to assistive tech without an authored aria-expanded attribute anywhere in the JSX. Verify when level 2 lands.

The native <select>'s dropdown is rendered by the OS/browser chrome, not a DOM node this component owns, so there is no popup element for an authored aria-controls to reference -- the association is entirely implicit in the native widget. Verify when level 2 lands.

Native <select> keeps DOM focus on the element itself and tracks the highlighted option internally; there is no authored aria-activedescendant, but the browser exposes the active option to assistive tech as part of the native combobox mapping. Verify when level 2 lands.
